zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：短线偏弱震荡，空头略占优势
zh.content:
SOL/USD当前维持窄幅整理，最新价格运行于81.96附近，日内波动较小，显示市场观望情绪明显。价格持续低于20日均线83.335，说明短线趋势仍偏空，反弹动能暂时不足，整体结构以弱势震荡为主。

从技术指标观察，MACD维持负值区域，表明空头动能仍未完全释放，短期存在继续回踩风险。虽然RSI数据暂缺，但结合价格表现与均线压制情况来看，多头买盘力量有限，市场尚未形成有效反转信号。

关键支撑位可关注81.80一线，若跌破则可能进一步测试80美元整数关口。上方阻力集中在82.30至83.30区域，尤其20日均线附近存在较强抛压，若无法放量突破，价格仍可能延续区间弱势运行。

交易策略方面，短线投资者宜保持谨慎，激进者可关注82美元附近高抛机会，并严格设置止损。稳健交易者建议等待价格重新站稳20日均线后再考虑顺势做多，以提高交易胜率与风险回报比。

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Solana remains under modest short-term pressure as price action trades below the 20-day simple moving average at 83.335. The current price near 81.96 reflects a weak intraday structure, with sellers maintaining control after repeated failures to reclaim resistance above the 82.30 region. Although the 24-hour decline is limited, the narrow trading range suggests declining momentum and cautious participation. Market sentiment appears neutral-to-bearish unless buyers can generate stronger volume and push SOL/USD back above its moving average resistance.

Momentum indicators also point toward softening conditions. The MACD reading at -0.484 indicates bearish momentum remains active, even though the absence of a confirmed signal line limits the strength of the interpretation. Price trading beneath the SMA(20) reinforces the idea of a short-term downtrend continuation. RSI data is unavailable, but current price compression near session lows suggests momentum is not yet oversold, leaving room for additional downside before a meaningful rebound develops.

From a technical perspective, immediate support is forming around 81.85, closely aligned with the session low near 81.87. A decisive break beneath this area could expose the psychological 81.00 level, followed by stronger demand around 79.80. On the upside, resistance is located near 82.30 initially, with a more important barrier around the SMA(20) at 83.33. A breakout above that zone would improve short-term sentiment and potentially shift momentum back toward 85.00.

Traders may consider maintaining a cautious stance until clearer directional confirmation emerges. Short-term bearish traders could favor selling rallies toward resistance while keeping tight risk controls above 83.35. Meanwhile, swing buyers may prefer waiting for either a confirmed rebound from support or a recovery above the moving average before entering long positions. Given the current compressed range and weakening momentum profile, disciplined position sizing and strict stop-loss management remain especially important in the near term.
